title: RHEL EXTRA's updated podman, port mapping isn't working
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
This morning I've checked the RHEL repositories and found that Podman 1.3.2 is added. After installing it I've got some issues which would be fixed by enabling rhel-7-server-beta-rpms I could install the latest shadow-utils and after some more bug fixes I could create a rootless container without port mapping but with port mapping, it is stuck on created container.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install RHEL 7.6, register, enable rhel-7-server-beta-rpms, rhel-7-server-extra-rpms, Execute a yum update and after install Podman. Enable user-namespaces, create a test user and add it to /etc/subid(subgid).

2. Fix first error ERRO[0000] container failed: cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers: internal libpod error. Fix this by installing a runc version higher then 1.0.0.83+

3. Solve the next issue ERRO[0000] error checking slirp4netns binary /usr/bin/slirp4netns: exit status 1, solve it by installing a newer version of slirp4netns.

**Describe the results you received:**
Rootless containers with port mapping will hang during start:

[test@rhel76 ~]$ podman --log-level=debug run -d -p 80 nginx
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db

DEBU[0001] Received container pid: 7524                 
DEBU[0001] Created container 81d786711dfe341aca46980c250a058ef5d8e17379ea8bb9b88380902b16c5d4 in OCI runtime 


**Describe the results you expected:**
A working container with port mapping.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.3.2

Go Version:         go1.10.8
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

comments.body: please file a bugzilla and close this
We make absolutely no promise that rootless Podman is working on RHEL7 right now. It will be working and supported with native packages as of 7.7, so I suggest you wait for that release
@mheon , Also the beta? Because I get exactly the same error in 7.7.
I think it should be - if you see this in the 7.7 Beta, I'd recommend opening a BZ, so we can potentially get a fixed build into the final release

The `podman` gid mapping problem appears to be reproducible with the [7.7 beta packages](https://access.redhat.com/downloads/content/69/ver=/rhel---7/7.7%20Beta/x86_64/packages). Refer to [buildah issue #1657](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1657) for the test case and steps to reproduce.

> [osboxes@osbox76 centos]$ rpm -q podman buildah runc
> podman-1.3.2-1.git14fdcd0.el7.x86_64
> buildah-1.8.2-2.gite23314b.el7.x86_64
> runc-1.0.0-59.dev.git2abd837.el7.x86_64

> [osboxes@osbox76 centos]$ buildah images
> REPOSITORY   TAG   IMAGE ID   CREATED   SIZE
> [osboxes@osbox76 centos]$ buildah containers
> CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
> [osboxes@osbox76 centos]$ buildah bud -f Dockerfile.build -t builder .
> STEP 1: FROM centos
> Getting image source signatures
> Copying blob 8ba884070f61 done
> Copying config 9f38484d22 done
> Writing manifest to image destination
> Storing signatures
> STEP 2: COPY build.sh .
> STEP 3: COPY chroot.sh /target/
> STEP 4: COMMIT builder
> Getting image source signatures
> Copying blob d69483a6face skipped: already exists
> Copying blob 35a05842a678 done
> Copying config cf6405a205 done
> Writing manifest to image destination
> Storing signatures
> --> cf6405a205ed4c7ba13a923b192923cee88c96dc7930da1603bbfed38b1aa7e9
> [osboxes@osbox76 centos]$ podman run --privileged -v $(pwd):/workspace builder /build.sh
> Error: container create failed: cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers
> : internal libpod error
>
please file a bugzilla if you hope to have this fixed.
